name: aligned_zero_size
reason: If alignment to size 0 is trivially true for all addresses, the spec lacks a guard against degenerate zero-size alignment

name: inv_empty_arch
reason: An architecture with zero layers is degenerate and should not satisfy the well-formedness invariant

name: upper_vaddr_no_progress
code:
proof fn phi_3_upper_vaddr_no_progress(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.upper_vaddr(layer, base) == base,
{
}
reason: If upper_vaddr equals base under a valid invariant, the layer covers zero address space, making the architecture useless

name: entry_base_collapses_indices
code:
proof fn phi_4_entry_base_collapses_indices(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat, j: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i < arch.num_entries(layer),
        j < arch.num_entries(layer),
        i != j,
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, i) == arch.entry_base(layer, base, j),
{
}
reason: If two distinct indices map to the same entry base, page table entries would alias and the address space partitioning is broken

name: entry_size_next_layer_vacuous
code:
proof fn phi_5_entry_size_next_layer_vacuous(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        arch.layers.len() > 1,
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(0) == arch.entry_size(1),
{
}
reason: If the first and second layer have equal entry sizes despite the decomposition property requiring entry_size(0) == entry_size(1) * num_entries(1) with num_entries > 1, the invariant would be contradictory or num_entries is forced to 1
